methods.description: Process for sampling ice wedges using a chainsaw to extract ice blocks for further analysis.

steps.description: The ice wedges were sampled as ice blocks using a chainsaw, in horizontal transects covering the entire ice-wedge width and partly including the ice-sand wedge section.
steps.searchable_summary: extract ice blocks from ice wedges using a chainsaw

methods.description: Preparation of horizontal thin sections from ice blocks for fabric, texture, gas, and stable isotope analyses.

steps.description: Horizontal thin sections were prepared using a traditional biological microtome for the clean ice and a diamond wire saw for the debris-laden ice.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare thin sections from ice blocks using microtome and diamond wire saw

methods.description: Determination of crystal size using two techniques: mean linear intercept method and counting entire crystals in a known area.

steps.description: The first method was the mean linear intercept method developed by Pickering, where boundaries N crossed by a random linear traverse of length L across the thin section was averaged over many traverses.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean grain diameter using mean linear intercept method

steps.description: The second method estimates the mean diameter by counting the number of entire crystals in a known area and assuming a circular cross-section.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate mean diameter by counting entire crystals in a known area
